summary: G5 templates + generation loop: prompts/dossier/*-v1.md + build_dossiers.py --generate (resumable, contract-validated)
description: 10 template files verbatim from design §1.3.2-3 (work-level wording), filename = prompt_version. Generate mode: DAG order over frozen plan; skip existing (work,field,span,model,prompt_version) rows; validate contracts (§1.1 JSON shapes; prose stages token-band ±20%); reject-retry x3; accepted-upstream inputs only; residual nav-clean imported from clean_bodies. Done: pilot run over one degenerate + one grouped + one large work produces contract-valid staged rows, resumably.

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: Pilot findings: (1) claude-code content filter deterministically blocks enuma-elish Part 1 L1 output ('400 Output blocked by content filtering policy', persists across retries) — engine now skips such nodes resumably; campaign gap-fill options: anthropic API provider or local provider for blocked nodes. (2) Span-label audit found collisions in 4 works (plotinus base-collapse from over-aggressive Section-tail strip; generic 'Section 1' across CH/republic/dionysius members) — fixed: lettered-only tail strip, merged-label prefix trim, cross-member disambiguation via member text_name (CH TOC now shows tractate titles). Plan re-frozen pre-campaign at 622 spans; only john-baptizer pilot rows were affected and were wiped + regenerated under the final plan.

resolution.note: 10 templates + generate mode complete. Pilot: full DAG exercised over heart-sutra-smaller (degenerate L2 path), gnostic-john-baptizer (grouped, 8 spans, all stages through reading_notes), enuma-elish (bare-format; Part 1 content-blocked and skipped resumably, Part 2 + structure generated, L2 correctly deferred at 1/2 L1s). Contract validation, reject-retry, idempotent re-runs, and accepted-upstream gating all demonstrated. ~45 staged rows produced.
